Replace GitHub Spark mockup with a functioning static site:

  tracking, AI suggestions

- Add GitHub Pages deployment workflow
- Rewrite README and all UI copy
- Clean up unused dependencies (d3, three, octokit, etc.)

commit.message:
Apply updated .gitignore - remove previously tracked build artifacts

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

Hide Anerkennung placeholder entries from course catalog

These are administrative recognition entries from the Excel source
(IN9954xx codes) — not real selectable courses. Filter them out by
name prefix so the catalog starts with actual course names.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

Fix filter freezing: useShallow + store-level toggleFilter action

Root cause: Zustand v5 concurrent rendering tears when multiple
usePlanStore() calls exist in the same component. Rapid clicks
hit React's batching limit and the UI freezes.

Fix:
- CourseCatalog: single useShallow selector for all filter slices
- FilterPanel: single useShallow selector for filters+actions
- store: add toggleFilter() action that reads current state
  internally (no stale closure possible — Zustand's set() always
  receives the latest state)
- toggleFilter replaces FilterPanel's local toggle() closure which
  could race on rapid repeated clicks

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

Fix filter logic: use per-field Zustand selectors in CourseCatalog

Previously used useMemo([filters, ...]) which could cache a stale
filters object reference. Now each filter field is read via its own
dedicated selector (usePlanStore(s => s.filters.X)), guaranteeing the
component re-renders and re-filters whenever any filter changes.
Also removed useMemo wrapper — filtering runs directly every render.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

commit.message:
Rewrite DnD: useDraggable+useDroppable only, no DragOverlay, direct CSS transform

- Remove DragOverlay (portal positioning was broken)
- Apply CSS.Translate.toString(transform) directly to dragging elements
- Remove SortableContext and useSortable from SemesterColumn
- Pure useDroppable columns, useDraggable for catalog + planned cards
- Listeners spread on full card for easy grab anywhere
- opacity:0.45 while dragging (keeps DOM node intact)
- Semester board always visible; filter/catalog/stats/validation collapsible

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

fix: DnD definitively fixed — eliminate duplicate droppable IDs

Root cause: desktop layout (hidden md:flex) and mobile layout (md:hidden)
were BOTH mounted in DOM simultaneously. dnd-kit registered the same
semesterId twice (once per layout). With two droppables sharing an ID,
collision detection returned ambiguous results and drops were lost.

Fix: JS-driven layout switch via useEffect + window.innerWidth.
Only ONE layout (desktop OR mobile) is ever mounted at a time.
Semester columns are registered as droppables exactly once.

Also removed all resize handles (VResizeHandle, HResizeHandle, useResize hook).
Layout uses fixed proportions: catalog = 44% height, board = flex-1.
SemesterGrid extracted as a stable top-level component to prevent
re-mount on parent re-renders.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

fix: completely rewrite DnD — smooth drag from anywhere on cards

Root causes fixed:
1. Custom ResizeAwarePointerSensor broke dnd-kit's internal activation logic → removed,
   using plain PointerSensor (resize handles are spatially separate, no conflict)
2. Catalog cards returned null when isDragging → dnd-kit lost the element's rect before
   DragOverlay could read it → ghost appeared at wrong position (the 'jump').
   Fix: keep element in DOM with opacity:0, DragOverlay positions correctly.
3. Drag listeners were only on the tiny GripVertical icon (~16px target).
   Fix: spread listeners on the entire card surface, stopPropagation on link/button children.
4. Activation distance reduced 8→5px for more responsive feel.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

fix: restore DnD + add unverifiable course warning

- Fix broken drag-and-drop: SemesterBoard was defined as a function component
  inside Home(), causing React to create a new component type every render,
  which unmounted/remounted all droppable SemesterColumns on each state change.
  Fix: inline the semester board JSX directly in both desktop and mobile slots.
- CourseCatalog: courses with vorlesungTerm=null now show an amber warning badge
  'Cannot verify if this course is currently offered'

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

Desktop:

- Right panel width draggable (200–500px) via grip handle on left edge
- Catalog/board vertical split draggable (100–650px) via grip handle between them
- Custom ResizeAwarePointerSensor: resize handles excluded from dnd-kit activation
- Footer (disclaimer + GitHub link) always pinned at bottom (shrink-0), visible regardless of filter state

Mobile (< md):
- Tab bar at bottom: Filter | Plan | Stats
- Plan tab: catalog (top 40%) + semester board (bottom) stacked
- Stats tab: Dashboard + Validation + Import/Export
- Active tab highlighted with primary color + top border indicator

- CourseCatalog: planned-course exclusion now matches on course id instead of code
  (fixes rare duplicate-code entries disappearing from catalog unexpectedly)

fix: semester import renumbering, smoother DnD, enriched Excel export

- Import: semesters always renumbered 1,2,3,… regardless of source IDs → no more gaps
- DnD: removed onDragOver state mutations (was causing 60fps re-renders + jumpiness)
  - All moves now committed in onDragEnd only
  - Increased activation distance to 8px (fewer accidental drags)
  - Switched to rectIntersection collision detection (more predictable)
  - DragOverlay dropAnimation=null (instant release, no ghost lag)
- Excel export now has 4 sheets:
  - Plan: full course list per semester
  - Validation: all degree requirements with status
  - Credits by Area: ECTS + course count per Schwerpunkt
  - Credits by Semester: total / theory / practical ECTS per semester

- Only apply CSS transform when actually non-null → no unnecessary style recalcs

commit.message:
[GPCAPIM-232] Add agent instructions (#100)

<!-- markdownlint-disable-next-line first-line-heading -->
## Description

Added some agent instructions and two starter-for-ten agents using the
[GitHub Copilot
docs](https://docs.github.com/en/copilot/how-tos/configure-custom-instructions/add-repository-instructions)

## Context

The use of coding agents has been enabled by NHSDigital org. These files
will give us a starting point to use agents to help maintain and improve
our repository.

## Type of changes

<!-- What types of changes does your code introduce? Put an `x` in all
the boxes that apply. -->

- [ ] Refactoring (non-breaking change)
- [ ] New feature (non-breaking change which adds functionality)
- [ ] Breaking change (fix or feature that would change existing
functionality)
- [ ] Bug fix (non-breaking change which fixes an issue)

## Checklist

<!-- Go over all the following points, and put an `x` in all the boxes
that apply. -->

- [x] I have followed the code style of the project
- [ ] I have added tests to cover my changes
- [ ] I have updated the documentation accordingly
- [ ] This PR is a result of pair or mob programming
- [ ] Exceptions/Exclusions to coding standards (e.g. #noqa or #NOSONAR)
are included within this Pull Request.

---

## Sensitive Information Declaration

To ensure the utmost confidentiality and protect your and others
privacy, we kindly ask you to NOT including [PII (Personal Identifiable
Information) / PID (Personal Identifiable
Data)](https://digital.nhs.uk/data-and-information/keeping-data-safe-and-benefitting-the-public)
or any other sensitive data in this PR (Pull Request) and the codebase
changes. We will remove any PR that do contain any sensitive
information. We really appreciate your cooperation in this matter.

- [x ] I confirm that neither PII/PID nor sensitive data are included in
this PR and the codebase changes.

---------

Signed-off-by: davidhamill1-nhs <109090521+davidhamill1-nhs@users.noreply.github.com>
Co-authored-by: Copilot <175728472+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

fix: PieChart double padding gap when a data item has value 0 (#7113) ## Description Fixes the double-padding bug in `Pie` charts where a zero-value data item combined with a non-zero `paddingAngle` causes a visibly larger gap between the adjacent non-zero sectors. **Root cause:** `computePieSectors` was storing `paddingAngle: 5` on zero-value sectors (same as non-zero ones). The `SectorsWithAnimation` rendering code reconstructs sector positions from scratch using each sector's stored `paddingAngle` as a cursor offset. Since a zero-value sector had `paddingAngle: 5`, the cursor advanced by 5° for the invisible sector, and then the next visible sector added another 5°, producing a 10° double gap instead of the expected 5°. **Fix:** Zero-value sectors now store `paddingAngle: 0` in `computePieSectors`, so the animation code does not advance the cursor for them. **Changes:** - `src/polar/Pie.tsx` — set `paddingAngle: 0` for zero-value sectors in `computePieSectors` - `test/state/selectors/pieSelectors.spec.tsx` — unit test verifying zero-value sectors have `paddingAngle: 0` - `test-vr/tests/PieChart.spec-vr.tsx` — VR test with data containing a `value: 0` entry and `paddingAngle={5}` - Updated Chromium baseline snapshot showing equal gaps between all visible sectors ## Related Issue Double padding on `PieChartWithPaddingAngle` when value is `0`. ## Motivation and Context When `paddingAngle` is set and a data item has `value: 0`, the gap between the two non-zero sectors surrounding the zero-value sector appears twice as wide as all other gaps. This is because the animation rendering code uses the stored `paddingAngle` field on each sector to re-derive positions, and a zero-value sector was incorrectly storing a non-zero `paddingAngle`, consuming extra space even though the sector itself has zero width. ## How Has This Been Tested? - Unit test in `test/state/selectors/pieSelectors.spec.tsx` directly asserts `paddingAngle === 0` for zero-value sectors and `paddingAngle === 5` for non-zero sectors - Playwright VR test run locally with `--update-snapshots` to regenerate the baseline; updated snapshot shows all three visible segments separated by equal 5° gaps - All 94 pie-related unit tests pass ## Screenshots (if appropriate): Three segments (400/300/200) with equal spacing — the gap where the zero-value segment sits is now the same width as all other gaps. ## Types of changes - [x] Bug fix (non-breaking change which fixes an issue) ## Checklist: - [ ] My change requires a change to the documentation. - [ ] I have updated the documentation accordingly. - [x] I have added tests to cover my changes. - [x] I have added a storybook story or VR test, or extended an existing story or VR test to show my changes
Original prompt > > ---- > > *This section details on the original issue you should resolve* > > Double padding on PieChartWithPaddingAngle when value is 0 > ## Do you want to request a feature or report a bug? > Bug > > ## What is the current behavior? > Currently recharts still adds a padding to sector with `0` value, which causes a double padding/spacing. > > ## If the current behavior is a bug, please provide the steps to reproduce and if possible a minimal demo of the problem. > https://jsfiddle.net/gm9rf6t2/ > > ## What is the expected behavior? > The padding/spacing between all sector should be the same. > > ## Which versions of Recharts, and which browser / OS are affected by this issue? Did this work in previous versions of Recharts? > <= v.1.50 > > > Add a VR test for this. How to reproduce: have a data array where at least one item has value 0. Add non-zero `paddingAngle` prop to Pie. > > ## Comments on the Issue (you are @copilot in this section) > > > >
- Fixes recharts/recharts#1687 --- 💡 You can make Copilot smarter by setting up custom instructions, customizing its development environment and configuring Model Context Protocol (MCP) servers. commit.message:
docs: comprehensive README with architecture, API reference, deployment guide

- Project overview with features table and tech stack
- Architecture diagram (frontend → Vercel proxy → Render → Supabase)
- Model architecture (MobileNetV3 + custom head)
- Full API reference with request/response examples for all 10 endpoints
- Local development guide (backend + frontend + Supabase setup)
- Environment variables reference table
- Full project structure tree
- Deployment guide (Vercel + Render + Docker)
- Drone/batch watcher usage
- Fine-tuning pipeline docs
- All 38 supported disease classes across 14 crops
- 8-language TTS support table
- Model performance metrics (val F1 = 0.9524)

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

Fix Gemini: add retry logic, auto-recovery, switch to gemini-2.0-flash

- Add 3-attempt retry with exponential backoff for transient failures
- Add rate limit detection with longer waits on 429 errors
- Auto-recover availability check every 5 minutes if previously down
- Switch default model from gemini-2.5-flash to gemini-2.0-flash (more stable)
- Update GEMINI_MODEL env var on Render

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

Implement the complete single-phase Valvatne-Blunt conductance model (#33)

* Implement Valvatne-Blunt single-phase conductance calculations and enhance shape factor methods

* Update conductance model to use 'valvatne_blunt' in benchmarks

* Add tests for Valvatne-Blunt conductance models and shape factor calculations

* Refactor conductance model references from 'valvatne_blunt_baseline' to 'valvatne_blunt' in tests

* Update conductance model in ipynbs to 'valvatne_blunt'

* Add MWE 14: Shape-factor hydraulic conductance comparison notebook

This notebook compares the default circular `generic_poiseuille` conductance with new shape-factor-aware Valvatne-Blunt models. It includes a synthetic Cartesian network and a real extracted Ketton rock network to evaluate the impact on absolute permeability estimation. Key findings include the recovery of the circular limit for throat-only shape-factor closure and the quantification of changes in throat conductance and absolute permeability for non-circular ducts.

* Update binary data files for voids network examples

* Update verification assets and enhance documentation for single-phase conductance models

- Updated binary images for permeability and porosity scatter plots.
- Modified CSV files for Stokes case results and limit representative data to reflect new calculations.
- Expanded background documentation with detailed explanations of the equivalent duct concept and shape-aware conductance models.
- Clarified hydraulic geometry fields and their significance in single-phase conductance models in the concepts documentation.
- Revised verification documentation to reflect the transition from `valvatne_blunt_baseline` to the complete `valvatne_blunt` model.

* Enhance tests for valvatne_blunt conductance model with additional validations and shape factor calculations

* Update section title from 'voids' to 'Overview' in documentation

* Add geometry repair options for Imperial export in network extraction and PoreSpy import

* Update docs

* Refactor tests: remove obsolete PoreSpy import tests and add new tests for Imperial export repairs

* Add external pnflow benchmark notebook for voids comparison

This notebook compares the `voids` workflow against a committed reference dataset produced with the Imperial College `pnextract` and `pnflow` workflow. It includes functionality to load reference data, run the `voids` extraction, and generate comparative metrics and visualizations for permeability and porosity. The results are saved for documentation and review.

* Refactor benchmark notebook: add project root detection, save figures, and improve plotting structure

* Add verification reports for OpenPNM cross-check and external pnextract/pnflow benchmark, including new figures and CSV results

* Validate shape_factor and throat area before overriding area in porespy importer (#34)

* Initial plan

* Validate shape_factor and throat area before overriding area in porespy importer

Co-authored-by: volpatto <5941226+volpatto@users.noreply.github.com>

* Guard computed area against overflow; fix test throat_conns alignment; add coverage tests

Co-authored-by: volpatto <5941226+volpatto@users.noreply.github.com>

---------

Co-authored-by: copilot-swe-agent[bot] <198982749+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

commit.message:
ci: add pre-commit hooks, GitHub Actions workflows, and apply ruff formatting

- Add .pre-commit-config.yaml with pre-commit-hooks (trailing whitespace,
  end-of-file, YAML/JSON check), ruff (lint + format), and a local
  locale-consistency hook
- Add scripts/check_locales.py to validate all locale files match en.json
- Add .github/workflows/pr.yml: lint, locale checks, and snap build for PRs
- Add .github/workflows/snap-edge.yml: publish to edge on every push to main
- Add .github/workflows/release.yml: manual workflow_dispatch to bump
  version in snapcraft.yaml, commit, tag, build, and publish to candidate;
  also creates a GitHub Release with auto-generated notes
- Fix pre-existing ruff issues: reorder imports in rag_indexer.py
  (E402), rename ambiguous variable in system_indexer.py (E741),
  remove unused variable (F841)
- Apply ruff format to all Python files

Analytics page (/dashboard/analytics):

New API routes:

fix: modal overlay now properly covers entire screen including sidebar

- Dialog overlay: z-50 → z-[500], opacity bg-black/50 → bg-black/60
- Dialog content: z-50 → z-[501] (above overlay)
- Sheet overlay/content: same z-index bump for consistency
- Chatbot: z-[99] → z-[200] (below dialog overlay but above page content)

modal backdrop. Sidebar appeared unmasked for the same reason.

commit.message:
feat: real backend — DB tables, 12 API routes, pages wired to live data

- Added 4 new DB tables: domains, mailboxes, contacts, org_settings
- Applied migration 0001 (domains, mailboxes, contacts, org_settings)
- Created 12 API routes with auth, typed responses, no mock data:
  /api/domains (GET, POST), /api/domains/[id] (GET, PUT, DELETE)
  /api/domains/[id]/verify (POST - real DNS: MX, SPF, DMARC, DKIM)
  /api/mailboxes (GET, POST), /api/mailboxes/[id] (GET, PUT, DELETE)
  /api/mailboxes/[id]/reset-password (POST)
  /api/contacts (GET, POST), /api/contacts/[id] (PUT, DELETE)
  /api/settings (GET auto-create defaults, PUT)
  /api/mail/send (POST - nodemailer), /api/mail/test (POST - SMTP+IMAP verify)
  /api/mail/inbox (GET - ImapFlow last 50 messages)
- Accounts page: real mailboxes + domains from API, add/delete wired
- Contacts page: real contacts from API, add/delete wired, no any types
- Admin page: domains CRUD + DNS verify, SMTP/IMAP config form, security policy all real
- Webmail: inbox from IMAP, compose sends via SMTP, unconfigured state handled
- Installed: nodemailer, imapflow, bcryptjs + types

commit.message:
refactor: centralize all UI colours into a single PALETTE dict

All 14 hex colour values are now defined once in the PALETTE dict
at the top of main.py. Every consumer — Rich Theme, Pygments style,
prompt_toolkit Style dicts, Panel borders, Table column styles,
Rich markup strings, progress bars, and console.print() calls —
references PALETTE['name'] instead of an inline hex literal.

Changing the entire colour scheme now requires editing only the
PALETTE dict.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

commit.message:
Replace in-memory storage with DB persistence for Availability and GuideVerification

- Add GuideVerificationSubmission and GuideVerificationDocument entities with EF
  configurations and indexes
- Add GuideBlockedDate and GuideRecurringPattern entities with EF configurations
  and indexes (unique constraint on guide+date, unique per-guide pattern)
- Register all four new DbSets in UrGuideContext and apply configurations
- Create migration 20260308000001_AddAvailabilityAndVerification
- Rewrite AvailabilityController: async actions backed by GuideBlockedDates and
  GuideRecurringPatterns; upsert logic for recurring patterns; IDs set explicitly
  before SaveChanges
- Rewrite GuideVerificationController: async actions backed by
  GuideVerificationSubmissions/GuideVerificationDocuments; new admin endpoints
  GET /pending, POST /{id}/approve, POST /{id}/reject
- Add VerificationDecisionRequest model (Reason + Notes)
- Update AdminService: GetPendingGuidesAsync and GetGuideVerificationDetailAsync
  read actual status and documents from DB; ProcessGuideVerificationAsync upserts
  a verification submission row on approve/reject

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

commit.message:
fix: disable Polar, fix DB driver, comment out Google sign-in button

- Switch db/drizzle.ts from neon-http to node-postgres (pg) for local DB
- Comment out Polar plugin in lib/auth.ts (invalid token 401)
- Comment out polarClient in lib/auth-client.ts
- Disable Google sign-in button on sign-in and sign-up pages
- Comment out authClient.customer.* calls in settings and manage-subscription
- Comment out authClient.checkout/portal in pricing-table
- Fix BETTER_AUTH_SECRET (was shell expression, now static value)

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>
